content.problemStatement:
Given an `N x M` integer matrix, print its element in spiral fashion (clockwise).

See the following example:



Output should be: 1 2 3 4 8 12 11 10 9 5 6 7


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two space separated integers NNN and MMM, denoting the no. of rows and columns in the input matrix.
Next NNN lines contains MMM space separated integers, the elements of the matrix.


Output Format

Output on N∗MN*MN∗M space separated integers, the elements of input matrix in spiral fashion.
constraints: The elements of the matrix are non-negative and won't exceed 1000
content.inputFormat: First line contains two space-separated integers N and M, denoting the number of rows and columns in the input matrix. Next N lines contain M space-separated integers, the elements of the matrix.
content.outputFormat: Output on a single line, N*M space-separated integers, the elements of the input matrix in spiral fashion.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The spiral traversal of the given matrix is 1, 2, 3, 4, 8, 12, 11, 10, 9, 5, 6, 7.
The spiral traversal of the given matrix is 1, 2, 4, 3.
The spiral traversal of the given matrix is 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

solution.approach: The key insight is to maintain four pointers (top, bottom, left, right) representing the current boundaries of the matrix. We then traverse the matrix in a spiral fashion by iterating over the elements in the following order: top row from left to right, right column from top to bottom, bottom row from right to left, and left column from bottom to top. We update the pointers accordingly after each iteration.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N*M) per test case, O(T*N*M) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is the input matrix itself.
